description: You can't talk about Atlanta without lemon pepper. This features a jumbo footlong hot dog, deep-fried and heavily tossed in a zesty, buttery lemon pepper seasoning.

The first-ever bar built literally on the hardwood in the NBA. Floor-seat ticket holders can stand mere feet from the sideline while bartenders mix high-end speakeasy-style cocktails.

A wild nod to a New England childhood classic. A double beef patty layered with creamy marshmallow Fluff, peanut butter, and crispy bacon.

Creamy Boursin and mozzarella cheeses rolled in pasta, coated in crushed Cool Ranch Doritos, and deep-fried into a crispy, molten snack.

Beyond the brisket, the sleeper hit here is the warm Jalapeño Cheddar Corn Muffins served with sweet honey butter.

A huge win for inclusive dining. This dedicated station is completely stocked with Gluten-Free Chicken Tenders & Wraps, Beyond Meat Burgers & Sausages, crispy cauliflower, sweet potato fries, and fresh kale salads.
NYC's legendary smashburger phenom has a permanent outpost inside the arena. Expect lacy-edged, ultra-crispy patties layered with melted American cheese, pickles, and their signature house sauce.
Bringing NYC's major "red sauce" trend to the basketball crowd.Perfectly cooked rigatoni tossed in a rich, velvety, and deeply savory spicy vodka sauce.
The definitive drink of the arena, inspired by the borough's ubiquitous street vendors. It’s a vibrant, spicy mango margarita finished with a heavy Tajín-crusted rim.

Pillowy, sweet Jamaican coco bread split open and stuffed to the brim with deeply seasoned island meats and spicy jerk flavors.
Low-and-slow authentic American BBQ paired with international street food. The BBQ Pulled Pork Sliders are a massive hit for the home crowd.

A high-energy, members-only lounge that infuses Brooklyn’s streetball and vibrant park culture with an upscale, all-inclusive buffet.

item_name: Brooklyn Market Presented by National Grid
description: The ultimate destination for diverse eats, featuring a rotating lineup of minority-owned neighborhood small businesses.

Standard Hot Dog, Pretzel Bites, Popcorn, Bottled Dasani Water, and Fountain Soda.

Southern BBQ meets the Midwest. The lines stay long for their Loaded Brisket Fries and 6-hour slow-smoked Pulled Pork Sandwich.

A sophisticated main concourse stop specializing in heavy-pouring, classic baseline cocktails like the Rocky's Bar Martini (using Ketel One and Carpano Bianco) and classic Manhattans.

The ultimate stunt dessert—graham cracker "chips" piled high with melted marshmallows, chocolate chunks, and a heavy drizzle of chocolate syrup.
Crispy, golden, and served piping hot with a side of dulce de leche.
Led by Chef Andres Dominguez, this upscale pre-game Chef’s Table boasts a prime layout of seasonal carving stations, gourmet charcuterie boards, and custom-paired wine flights.

description: A towering, intensely savory masterpiece featuring three smashed beef patties, layers of crispy bacon, and molten American cheese on a toasted bun.
